title: AWaC <product>/agent-stack/deploy.yml
description: Per-product deploy spec consumed by the universal `deploy_product` workflow router. Each product declares the components that get deployed, the order, and which target each one uses. The target sub-block is interpreted by the topical workflow for that target (e.g. deploy_to_odoo_sh, deploy_to_aws_ecs).

product.description: Product slug (lowercased, no `get` prefix). Must match the product field in the agent-stack's awac.yml.

name.description: Stable identifier of the component. Snake_case or kebab-case.

target.description: Target type. The router workflow delegates to the matching topical workflow (deploy_to_<target>.md).

repo.description: GitHub repo (org/name) that holds the source. Optional when the component is cross-repo.

requires_human_approval.description: When true, the deploy router PAUSES before pushing this component and waits for an explicit human ack. Default true — opt out only for low-risk targets like preview deploys.

pre_steps.description: Workflow IDs that MUST pass before the deploy proceeds. e.g. run_tests_local, run_odoo_tests_docker_wsl, terraform_plan.

promote_after_pass.description: After the target acks success, push to these additional locations. Used for the Odoo flow (push to odoo_sh staging first, then to canonical repo branch).

rollback_window_minutes.description: How many minutes the agent should monitor post-deploy for rollback signals.

require_pass_on.description: Optional: only promote after this target acks. e.g. 'odoo_sh'.
